title: Borani Banjan

ingredients.name: brinjal (aubergine), sliced into 1 cm rounds
visual_checks: Salt the rounds and leave 10 minutes before frying to draw out moisture — this is especially important for large, water-heavy brinjals.
ingredients.notes: Salt the rounds and leave 10 minutes before frying to draw out moisture — this is especially important for large, water-heavy brinjals.

subs.name: Courgette (zucchini) rounds fried the same way give a milder, slightly sweeter variation — reduce frying time to 2 minutes per side.

ingredients.name: thick plain curd (yogurt)
visual_checks: Use full-fat, thick yogurt; whisk smooth. Hung curd or Greek-style yogurt works best as a topping.
ingredients.notes: Use full-fat, thick yogurt; whisk smooth. Hung curd or Greek-style yogurt works best as a topping.

subs.name: Thick coconut yogurt for a dairy-free version — the tangy cooling effect is preserved and the dish becomes vegan.

ingredients.name: garlic cloves (3 for tomato sauce, 2 crushed into yogurt)
visual_checks: Mash the 2 garlic cloves for yogurt very finely or grate them on a Microplane for a smooth, pungent garlic yogurt.
ingredients.notes: Mash the 2 garlic cloves for yogurt very finely or grate them on a Microplane for a smooth, pungent garlic yogurt.

ingredients.name: cumin seeds
visual_checks: A small amount added to the tomato sauce rounds the flavour.
ingredients.notes: A small amount added to the tomato sauce rounds the flavour.

ingredients.name: fresh mint leaves, for garnish
visual_checks: Dried mint can be used if fresh is unavailable; rub between fingers before scattering to activate the aroma.
ingredients.notes: Dried mint can be used if fresh is unavailable; rub between fingers before scattering to activate the aroma.

subs.name: Dried mint rubbed between the palms provides the same aroma at a fraction of the cost — use 1 tsp.

ingredients.name: cooking oil
visual_checks: Divided between frying the brinjal and making the tomato sauce.
ingredients.notes: Divided between frying the brinjal and making the tomato sauce.

steps.text: Sprinkle the brinjal rounds with salt on both sides and spread on a tray. Leave for 10 minutes — the salt will draw moisture to the surface, which you then pat dry with a cloth. Drying the surface is what allows the brinjal to fry rather than steam.

Heat 4 tbsp oil in a wide frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the brinjal rounds in a single layer — do not crowd the pan. Fry for 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden-brown and cooked through. Work in batches if needed. Drain on paper towels and season lightly with salt.

In the same pan (wipe it clean and add 1 tbsp fresh oil), cook the cumin seeds (if using) and 3 minced garlic cloves over medium heat for 30 seconds. Add the chopped tomatoes, turmeric, red chilli powder, and a pinch of salt. Cook for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have completely broken down and the sauce is thick, dark, and oily.

Prepare the garlic yogurt: whisk the curd smooth in a bowl. Add the 2 crushed garlic cloves and a good pinch of salt. Mix well and taste — the yogurt should be cool, creamy, and pungently garlicky. Chill in the refrigerator while you assemble the dish.

Spread the garlic yogurt in an even layer on a wide, shallow serving plate or platter. Arrange the fried brinjal rounds on top in a single overlapping layer. Spoon the tomato sauce over the brinjal — you want some yogurt visible around the edges and the sauce centred on the brinjal.

Scatter fresh mint leaves and coriander (if using) over the top and serve immediately. Borani banjan is eaten at room temperature — the contrast between the warm sauce and the cool yogurt is an intentional feature of the dish. Serve with Afghan naan or bread for scooping.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: curd → Thick coconut yogurt for a dairy-free version — the tangy cooling effect is preserved and the dish becomes vegan.; brinjal → Courgette (zucchini) rounds fried the same way give a milder, slightly sweeter variation — reduce frying time to 2 minutes per side.; mint → Dried mint rubbed between the palms provides the same aroma at a fraction of the cost — use 1 tsp.
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Cook identically to South Indian brinjal fry or baingan bharta preparation stages — the shallow-frying technique is exactly the same. Thick hung curd (chakka dahi) available at Indian dairy stores is the closest to Afghan-style thick yogurt. Fresh mint (pudina) is readily available in Indian markets. The tomato sauce is essentially a simple tomato masala — familiar to any Indian home cook. Serve with plain naan or tandoori roti.
